character.name: Dr. Fedu Nimum (Nim)

attacks.name: Light Pistol
attacks.notes: Simple blaster starting option; uses Dexterity for ranged attack and damage. Resource hook: depletes 1 shot from the loaded power cell.

depletes.notes: Spend 1 shot/charge from the light pistol's loaded power cell.

attacks.name: Vibrodagger
attacks.notes: Emergency cutting tool; sheet uses Dexterity via finesse/thrown-friendly build.

resources.name: Spare Power Cells
resources.notes: Spare cells only. One additional power cell is modeled as loaded inside the Light Pistol.

customRolls.name: AC Reference: Infused Fiber Armor + Shield
customRolls.notes: AC 16 = fiber armor 12 + Dex mod 2 + Infuse Item 1 + light shield generator 1.

customRolls.name: Tech Save DC Reference
customRolls.notes: Reference only: Tech save DC 16 normally; DC 17 while using the Gadgeteer Harness with Integrated Inhibitor.

customRolls.name: Tech Attack Modifier Reference
customRolls.notes: Reference only: ranged tech attack is +8. If Integrated Targeter is later installed, change this note to +9.

customRolls.name: Computer Slicing / Technology
customRolls.notes: Technology check for computers; Nim's Bith Programmer trait and Techie-style expertise are represented here.

customRolls.name: Close Investigation
customRolls.notes: Bith Detail Oriented: roll with advantage when scouring for details within 5 feet.

customRolls.name: Hearing/Smell Perception
customRolls.notes: Bith Keen Hearing and Smell: roll with advantage when the check involves hearing or smell.

customRolls.name: Potent Aptitude Die
customRolls.notes: Bonus action to give an ally within 60 feet a d6 for one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within 10 minutes. Uses refresh on short or long rest at Engineer 5.

customRolls.name: Projected Barrier: Environmental
customRolls.notes: Bonus action; spends Potent Aptitude. Barrier HP vs one chosen damage type: acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, or sonic.

depletes.notes: Projected Barrier spends one Potent Aptitude use.

customRolls.name: Projected Barrier: Physical
customRolls.notes: Bonus action; spends Potent Aptitude. Barrier HP vs one chosen physical damage type: energy, ion, or kinetic. Half engineer level rounded down is already entered as +2.

depletes.notes: Projected Barrier spends one Potent Aptitude use.

customRolls.name: Shocking Barrier Retaliation
customRolls.notes: When a creature protected by Nim's barrier is damaged by a creature within 5 feet, the protected creature can deal this lightning damage to the attacker.

customRolls.name: Sentry Turret Damage
customRolls.notes: A deployed sentry targets a hostile creature within 10 feet at end of Nim's turn. Target makes Dex save DC 17 or takes damage and gains 1 slowed level.

customRolls.name: Deploy Sentry Turret
customRolls.notes: Deploy one sentry turret from the Gadgeteer Harness. The turret's later damage roll does not spend another turret.

customRolls.name: Oil Spill
customRolls.notes: Action; cast oil slick without tech points or concentration. Recharges on short or long rest.

depletes.notes: Use the Oil Spill contraption; refreshes on short or long rest.

customRolls.name: Ion Blast
customRolls.notes: At-will tech power. One target within 60 feet, or two within 5 feet of each other, makes Dex save DC 17 or takes ion damage.

customRolls.name: Kolto Pack
customRolls.notes: 1st-level tech power; emergency bonus-action healing. Costs 2 tech points.

customRolls.name: Repair Droid
customRolls.notes: Touch a droid or construct to restore hit points. Techie can cast once without tech points; normally costs 2 tech points at 1st level.

depletes.notes: Spend the once-per-long-rest free Repair Droid use.

customRolls.name: Tactical Barrier
customRolls.notes: Bonus action; concentration up to 10 minutes; target within 60 feet gains +2 AC. Costs 2 tech points.

customRolls.name: Energy Shield
customRolls.notes: Reaction defense; when hit by an attack, gain +5 AC until the start of Nim's next turn. Costs 2 tech points.

customRolls.name: Hold Droid
customRolls.notes: 2nd-level tech power; one droid or construct within 60 feet makes Con save DC 17 or is paralyzed. Concentration up to 1 minute. Costs 3 tech points.

customRolls.name: Lock
customRolls.notes: 2nd-level tech power; touch a door, window, gate, chest, or entryway to lock it and increase break/pick DC by 10. Costs 3 tech points.

customRolls.name: Invisibility to Cameras
customRolls.notes: 3rd-level tech power; up to four creatures become undetectable to electronic sensors and cameras. Concentration up to 1 minute. Costs 4 tech points.

depletes.notes: Cast Invisibility to Cameras at 3rd level.

customRolls.name: Scramble Interface
customRolls.notes: 3rd-level tech power prepared for Separatist droids and systems. Use Tech save DC 17 while harness-focused. Costs 4 tech points.

customRolls.name: Slicer's Kit Check
customRolls.notes: Use for console intrusion, encrypted archives, data recovery, and droid/security systems when expertise applies.

customRolls.name: Security Kit Check
customRolls.notes: Use for locks, access panels, and physical security bypasses when tool proficiency applies.

customRolls.name: Tinker's Implements Check
customRolls.notes: Use for repair, calibration, jury-rigging, and small-device modification.

customRolls.name: Gadgeteer's Implements Check
customRolls.notes: Use for Gadgeteer Harness adjustments, turret/barrier emitter work, and contraption maintenance.

character.notes: Bith Gadgeteer Engineer built for a level 5 Clone Wars Republic mission. Species traits: speed 30, Detail Oriented, Keen Hearing and Smell, Musician, Programmer. Languages: Galactic Basic, Bith, binary/droid-oriented mission language by GM approval. Saving throws: Constitution and Intelligence. Skills: Investigation, Technology, Piloting, Lore, Medicine. Technology is marked expertise for app simplicity; if your GM wants Bith Programmer to apply only to computer-related Technology checks, set technology.expertise to false and use the Computer Slicing custom roll instead. Engineer 5 core values: 12 tech powers known, 10 base tech points, max power level 3rd, Potent Aptitude d6, 5 modification slots. Nim is using 4 harness modifications, one over proficiency bonus, so tech point max is entered as 9. Harness modifications assumed: Integrated Inhibitor, Sentry Turret, Oil Spill, Shocking Barrier. Tech powers known: Ion Blast, Mending, On/Off, Analyze, Energy Shield, Kolto Pack, Smoke Cloud, Tactical Barrier, Repair Droid, Hold Droid, Lock, Invisibility to Cameras, Scramble Interface. Combat gear: infused fiber armor, light shield generator, light pistol, vibrodagger, wristpad, tinker's implements, slicer's kit, gadgeteer's implements, and Gadgeteer Harness. Personality: precise, over-formal, nasal technical specialist who protects allies with barriers, complains politely about unsafe equipment, and treats droid warfare as an engineering hygiene problem. Inventory update: spent 4,995 of 5,000 credits on infused fiber armor, light shield generator, security kit, pocket scrambler, holotrace device, comm jammer, repair kit, 2 medpacs, traumakit, and 12 additional power cells. Combat AC is now 16 from infused fiber armor plus light shield generator. Power cell resource is 14 total including 2 starting cells. Basic inventory update: added Engineer starting gear, Scientist background gear, Gadgeteer-specific tools, dungeoneer's pack contents, and Nim's visual/personal gear: magnifying monocular, tiny-tool bandolier, bright pink insulated gloves, Aurebesh warning tags, diagnostic probes, blank datacards, spare parts, dust wraps, and Republic contractor badge. Resource-depletion update: actions and inventory items now use depletes/depletionOptions hooks. The light pistol contains a loaded power cell tracked as an item-contained resource; spare power cells are tracked separately.

inventory.name: Infused Fiber Armor
inventory.notes: Light armor. Nim infuses this armor after a long rest. With Dex +2, armor gives AC 15 before shield.

inventory.name: Light Shield Generator
inventory.notes: Adds +1 AC while active/equipped. Combined with infused fiber armor, Nim has AC 16.

inventory.name: Security Kit
inventory.notes: For locks, access panels, and physical security bypasses.

inventory.name: Pocket Scrambler
inventory.notes: Encrypts or protects communications during sensitive Republic operations.

containedResources.name: Internal Power Cell
containedResources.notes: Optional app hook: the scrambler can be treated as containing one power cell. Deplete manually when the GM decides its cell is exhausted.

inventory.name: Holotrace Device
inventory.notes: Used to trace holographic transmissions, signals, or archive traffic.

inventory.name: Comm Jammer
inventory.notes: Local interference tool for disrupting Separatist coordination.

containedResources.name: Internal Power Cell
containedResources.notes: Optional app hook: the jammer can be treated as containing one power cell. Deplete manually when the GM decides its cell is exhausted.

inventory.name: Repair Kit
inventory.notes: Field repair supplies for droids, constructs, and damaged mission gear.

inventory.name: Medpac
inventory.notes: Emergency medical supplies for organic allies.

inventory.name: Traumakit
inventory.notes: Emergency stabilization kit.

inventory.name: Spare Power Cells
inventory.notes: 13 spare power cells. One additional power cell is loaded inside the Light Pistol and tracked as that item's contained resource.
inventory.source: Engineer starting gear + purchased mission gear

inventory.name: Light Pistol
inventory.notes: Simple blaster sidearm; represented in attacks as Light Pistol.

containedResources.name: Loaded Power Cell
containedResources.notes: Loaded cell for the light pistol. Each Light Pistol attack depletes 1 shot. Reloading can spend 1 spare power cell to refill this to max; adjust max if your table uses a different reload value.

depletes.notes: Normally handled by the Light Pistol attack entry.

inventory.name: Vibrodagger
inventory.notes: Emergency cutting tool and backup melee weapon; represented in attacks as Vibrodagger.

inventory.name: Combat Suit
inventory.notes: Original Engineer starting armor, retained as a backup. Nim currently wears infused fiber armor instead.

inventory.name: Wristpad
inventory.notes: Wrist-mounted interface for tech powers, slicing notes, diagnostics, and field references.

containedResources.name: Internal Power Cell
containedResources.notes: Optional app hook for battery tracking; most tables can ignore this unless power management matters.

inventory.name: Tinker's Implements
inventory.source: Engineer class proficiency and starting gear
inventory.notes: Core Engineer tool set for repair, calibration, small-device work, and improvised fixes.

inventory.name: Slicer's Kit
inventory.notes: Computer intrusion, data recovery, security bypasses, and archive access. Pair with Nim's Technology expertise.

inventory.name: Gadgeteer's Implements
inventory.notes: Specialized implements for the Gadgeteer Harness, barrier emitters, turret maintenance, and contraption tuning.

inventory.name: Gadgeteer Harness
inventory.notes: Enhanced harness and tech focus. Currently has 4 modifications installed; one over proficiency bonus, so Nim's tech point maximum is reduced by 1.

inventory.name: Dungeoneer's Pack
inventory.notes: Standard practical field pack for entering hostile facilities, tunnels, and battle-station interiors. Glow rods and rations are also tracked as resources.

inventory.name: Datapad
inventory.notes: Research notes, archive index, Republic credentials, diagnostic logs, and unnecessarily precise checklists.

inventory.name: Research Book: Artificial Intelligence
inventory.notes: Nim's chosen field notes for AI, droid heuristics, and Separatist automation quirks.

inventory.name: Common Clothes
inventory.notes: Plain spare clothes, folded with upsetting precision.

inventory.name: Spotless Technician's Utility Coat
inventory.notes: Tidy pale field coat worn over armor and harness; somehow kept clean despite Geonosian dust.

inventory.name: Bright Pink Insulated Gloves
inventory.notes: Distinctive insulated gloves for live-circuit work. Also Nim's most visible fashion crime.

inventory.name: Clipped-On Magnifying Monocular
inventory.notes: Mechanical magnifier clipped over one eye for precision inspection and judgmental staring.

inventory.name: Bandolier of Tiny Color-Coded Tools
inventory.notes: Cross-body bandolier with labeled micro-tools, probes, couplers, tweezers, calibration pins, and tiny drivers.

inventory.name: Aurebesh Warning Tags
inventory.notes: Small dangling labels attached to harness modules. The NOT A SNACK tag is not explained.

inventory.name: Diagnostic Probes and Sensor Leads
inventory.notes: Clip leads, micro-probes, continuity testers, and sensor taps for consoles, droids, and sealed machinery.

inventory.name: Blank Datacards
inventory.notes: For copying logs, recovered archives, sensor data, droid diagnostics, and classified things Nim definitely labels.

inventory.name: Spare Parts Pouch
inventory.notes: Basic field repair bits for the harness, wristpad, blaster contacts, and angry doors.

inventory.name: Geonosis Dust Wraps and Filter Plugs
inventory.notes: Simple dust protection for vents, gloves, cuffs, and exposed tool ports during sand-heavy operations.

inventory.name: Republic Technical Contractor Badge
inventory.notes: Authorizes Nim to be present with the Republic entourage, at least according to Nim's very firm interpretation.

inventory.name: Glow Rods

depletes.notes: Activate or expend one glow rod.

inventory.notes: Tracked as a resource so the app can decrement uses.

inventory.name: Field Rations
inventory.notes: Tracked as a resource so the app can decrement days.

credits.notes: Purchased mission gear package; fiber armor is infused after a long rest.
